content: Just as high school language arts curricula teach structured written reflection as a tool to sharpen explicit reasoning rather than just passive self-examination, current LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by borrowing the scaffolded, language learning-style reflection framework from the original language meaning of reflexion. This framework would guide LLMs to explicitly break down mistake patterns, rephrase incorrect reasoning in plain terms, and connect new errors to past lessons the same way students practice reflective writing to build critical thinking skills over time.
